name: Insect bites and stings
summary: Midges, mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, wasps, bees. Usually minor — but watch for anaphylaxis.
recognition: Severe reaction: swelling spreading, breathing difficulty — see Anaphylaxis

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if signs of anaphylaxis appear: facial swelling, breathing problems, widespread rash.

steps: Treat as "if it's hot, cool it".
Cold cloths or an ice pack (wrapped) to the area.
See a doctor if symptoms persist beyond a few hours.
Bee leaves the sting + venom sac behind in the skin.
Scrape it out IMMEDIATELY with a hard edge (credit card, blunt knife).
Don't squeeze it — that pumps more venom in.
Cool the area with a cold pack.

warnings.text: Around 3 in 100 people have an allergic reaction to bee stings. If facial/throat swelling or breathing difficulty starts — treat as Anaphylaxis.
